parsed.rationale: Region C displays a significantly higher concentration of fiber bundles compared to the other labeled areas. The fibers in this region are tightly packed and form a dense, thick band running horizontally across the center of the brain. In contrast, regions A, B, and D show fibers that are more spread out or oriented vertically. Therefore, Region C clearly exhibits the highest density.
